hotel_name: Khách sạn Haven Hut Riverside (Haven Hut Riverside Hotel)

reviews.title: stay away, and many police visa raids
reviews.text: Cigarette smoke everywhere, sound proofing problems and yes Vietnamese police raids checking for Visas at midnight - beating on doors and acting like they have no respect. I had recently has surgery and was sedated and sleeping so hard I did not hear the knock on my door at midnight for 15 minutes. But they kept knocking every 2-3 minutes over and over and over, forcing me out of bed to hand over my passport. I stayed in this hotel and police raided it twice in less than 2 weeks, meaning hotel must not be keeping accurate records? Beware...

reviews.title: Very disappointed.
reviews.positives: Hot water service system.
reviews.negatives:
1) Air con leaking water at all times.
2) Clothes/underwear from previous customer  left behind in the bathroom behind the closing door.
3) The young girl receptionist (morning time) is terrible. She was not concentrated to provide the service rather than focus on her own mobile. 
4) We are foreign tourists required assistance but she showed unwillingness to assist. Her attitude was arrogance and ignorance which was unacceptable.

reviews.title: Very bad and noisy hotel
reviews.positives: The room was clean
reviews.negatives:
I arrived there after a long bus journey and before a long flight. I told the receptionist that i am looking for a very quiet room , at first she show me very tiny room in the second floor. I ask for a higher floor room in the hope that it will more quite. I booked a room in the 7 floor. I find out avontily that they are doing a construction work in this floor dreling and nooking on the ceiling and walls. It was super noisy at the afternoon and the next morning i was able to sleep and i got a horrible headache before my over see flight i complen but they done nothing.
The t v in the room doesn't work, i ask few times that they will fix it , every time they say they are coming.... Or in half an hour. But again nothing happened avontily they said that t.v is old. If the t.v was working i was able at least to fut some music to prevent some of the horrible noise of the construction job that they done inside the hotel.
I book the hotel throw booking com. They total price was about 550,000 dong.  When i arrived the reception ask at beginning for 1.3 million ( she didn't know that i booked already) then she say the price is 600,000 dong because of vat , i take a look at the email from booking com and i find out that the vat was not included in the original price that publish. 
In short i have very negative opinion about this hotel they only care about money and nothing about service honestly or costumer service i recommend to everyone not to stay in this over price and run down place

reviews.title: Bad Hotel

reviews.title: Disgusting
reviews.text:
Bed sheets dirty, floor dirty, cups dirty plus staff was very unfriendly. Booked a room with city view gave me tiny room with no view.  Asked to change the room due to how dirty it was, gave me the room next door. Which wasn't any better then the previous one. I was so frustrated with the quality of hygiene and tiredness for travelling. I ended up having a mental breakdown. While slept on a dirty bed to find insect crawling on my bed in the morning.
I thought I was being paranoid that was being beaten but there were bugs on the bed. How dirty the room has to be to have bugs in it? I ended up checking out 7 days earlier then the initial booking. I had to pay double the price to find another accommodation because of valentine's and lack of places being available. I've no words to express my frustration.

reviews.title: District 4 Dwelling
reviews.text:
Miss Linh was nice at check-in and check-out. Henry was super friendly and pleasant during the afternoon/evening shift. I wish I could give a better rating, but nothing else here was great.

Pros: A/C and internet worked well. Friendly staff.

Cons: The cleanliness was poor - there were hairs on the toilet seat and on the floor / surfaces. There were blue pen marks on  tbed sheets. Terrible noise insulation. Hairs on the bed. Smoke smell coming through bathroom fan. No housekeeping, but they did enter and take something from the fridge. Construction starting early in the morning.

reviews.title: Maybe Better Than A Backpacker's Hostel
reviews.text: They insist on checking-in after 2pm, yet no towels upon entering room. Only discovered after unpacking. Asked for the towels at front desk, only to receive them from a very unhappy housekeeping women. And she was spiteful not to give me the smaller ones. Luckily the 2nd & 3rd day I don't see her again & the other cleaners were a lot nicer. Dirty & faulty kettle that keep boiling if you don't turn it off manually & full of dirt coming from the cover. I rinse it for more than 20 times & there's still too much dirt coming off the kettle. Just not safe to use. Hugh housefly in the room too & sticky toilet tiles. Need to walk a bit to a convenient store, otherwise can buy from local shops that doesn't speak a word of English. Laundry service is hotel is way overpriced, charge by the piece. No tourist sights nearby, need to Grab car to go anywhere. No wonder only this hotel has available rooms on 1st Jan, which is the reason I booked. Surely it's not a good choice.

reviews.title: Very good

reviews.title: Not recommended.
reviews.text:
I landed in Ho Chi Minh City early in the morning, so I needed to find a hotel that allows 24-hour check-in. I sent an email to the hotel to confirm in advance but did not receive a reply. 

There were steps at the hotel entrance, and no one helped me with my luggage. The staff were not friendly. 

The room was very small, and the bathroom was also tiny. One window had no curtains, and I was bright awake very early. The bedding was not clean, the pillow was yellow and damp. 

The labeling of paid items was unclear; there were charges for water and cola in the fridge. 

The hotel was located quite far from the bustling city center, and there were no shops open around at night. While waiting for a car, there was a drunk man across the street shouting, which was somewhat scary.

reviews.title: Excellent

reviews.negatives: I was just a little surprised that the bathroom was separated from the "other" room by glass. There was also a small opening, but it wasn't an issue for privacy at all.

The cleaning ladies should clean the water that they clean the floors or change cleaners that do a better job.

Water pressure is good with all toiletries provided. Room size is big enough but with no table and chairs and was told not to eat inside room. Foods aplenty about 50 metres to the right of hotel. For those who like local foods and don't mind the deserted street after 10pm( I came back around this timing), it is recommended.

reviews.title: Office
reviews.text: I have been here a few times. I have stayed at both the Q1 and Q4 branches at night. In my opinion, the room is acceptable, but compared to the price I booked directly, it is not quite worth it. The room is newly built, but I find it quite dirty, with some cement on the walls, the door still has lime on it, and there are spots that seem to be leaking or due to a lack of paint, the paint has been covered with another color, making it look a bit blotchy. A few times I have stayed before, the room had a vanity mirror, but because I checked in late today, I was assigned a room that is not as convenient as before, with no mirror, no chair, no paper towels, there's a balcony but it's quite dirty and dusty. This time the internet connection in the room is very weak, and I almost had to use my personal 4G. This time the room I booked on the app was slightly more expensive than previous times, but the service received is considerably worse. I hope to be considered for improvements in the future. I quite like the room design and hope that my future experiences at the hotel will be more comfortable! Thank you.

reviews.text:
Advantages:
The location in District 4 makes it easy to move to District 1 and District 8 for nearby outings.

Disadvantages:
- The parking ramp is steep, and there is no place to park cars.
- The room is not soundproof, there is no wardrobe for hanging clothes, and the bathroom has a cabinet that is completely locked and unusable.
- The floors are very dusty as they have just been built and not cleaned properly, you must wear slippers in the room or your feet will be completely black.
- The bathroom has only one light, so the lighting is weak, and the shower area is very dim with low water pressure.

Rating: 6/10

reviews.title: Need to carefully consider
reviews.text:
Stayed 2 nights at Ibis. The staff were very friendly and helpful, but there are a couple of things to be aware of.

The bathroom had a strong smell. The team tried to fix it and said it might be due to high tide, but it didn’t improve. They offered to change my room on the second day, but I didn’t move because it was inconvenient to pack and unpack again.

Parking is also limited. Spots are not reserved by license plate, so if you go out and return late, you might have to park in the small alley beside the hotel. It’s still within the hotel area, but not convenient, and you may need to wake up to move your car if someone is blocked.

Overall: great staff, but bathroom smell and parking can be an issue.

Good value if you get a deal. Bring some air freshener or essential oils for the smell.

reviews.title: THE HOTEL IS VERY CLEAN
reviews.text:
- Advantages:
+ The hotel is clean, and the hallways always smell nice. Other amenities are also quite new, and there is a spacious motorbike parking area in the basement.
+ Friendly and enthusiastic staff, who are attentive to guests.
+ Convenient location with many dining options nearby.

Disadvantages:
+ Sound insulation is not very good.
+ The lighting is too dim; the yellow lights are not bright enough for working. During the day, you can open the window, but at night it’s a challenge. The dim lights make it hard to see in the mirror, so I, as a girl, can't apply makeup properly.
+ No doormat in front of the bathroom door.
+ There is no chair in the room; a stool could be placed at the vanity table, which has a mirror but no chair, unfortunately.

=> In summary, I am quite satisfied with my experience here. The price is very reasonable, and I prioritize cleanliness in accommodation, which this place provides, so if I have the opportunity, I would come back here.

The staff were very friendly and helpful when I had an issue with my power adapter (you can buy one in a very small shop next-door!). Originally my taxi driver took me to the wrong hotel (there is another Haven Hut in the centre, make sure you select the right one!). The staff at the sister hotel were also super helpful. Overall no complaints!

reviews.title: A Hidden Riverside Gem in the Heart of Saigon
reviews.text:
A Hidden Riverside Gem in the Heart of Saigon

Haven Hut Riverside Hotel is the perfect blend of city energy and riverside tranquility. Tucked away from the hustle and bustle of Hồ Chí Minh City, yet close enough to reach the main attractions with ease, it offers a peaceful escape without sacrificing convenience.

The rooms are spotless, comfortable, and well-equipped with everything you need—plush beds, cold air conditioning, and strong Wi-Fi. Many have views of the river, making mornings especially relaxing with a cup of coffee in hand.

The staff here are the true highlight. They’re warm, welcoming, and always eager to help—whether it’s arranging transportation, recommending hidden food spots, or simply making sure you’re comfortable.

If you’re looking for a calm riverside stay in Saigon with genuine hospitality, Haven Hut Riverside Hotel is an excellent choice.
